sanskrit: अक्षरं ब्रह्म परंमं स्वभावोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते । भूतभावोद्भवकरो विसर्गः कर्मसंज्ञितः ॥
transliteration: akṣaraṃ brahma paramaṃ svabhāvo 'dhyātmam ucyate | bhūta-bhāvodbhava-karo visargaḥ karma-saṃjñitaḥ ||
literal_meaning: The imperishable Absolute is called Brahman; one’s own essential nature is called the self; the creative force that brings material bodies into being is termed karma.
contextual_meaning: Krishna clarifies three core concepts: the eternal conscious ground (Brahman), the unchanging essence of the individual (svabhāva), and the psychophysical process that keeps the person cycling through embodied identities (karma).
psychological_state: Arjuna experiences a moment of epistemic clarity—his confusion about who he really is beneath social roles and bodily labels begins to dissolve, replaced by an intuitive grasp of himself as immutable awareness.
trigger_scenario: A professional on the verge of burnout realizes, 'I am not my résumé, my body, or my accomplishments; I am the awareness that has been watching all of these change.'
anchor_text: Know the eternal, your true self, and let action flow without clinging.
